name: benchmark
tagline: Performance regression detection for web pages: baselines Core Web Vitals and bundle size, flags regressions on each PR.
verdict: Thorough and deterministic — explicit numeric regression thresholds, worked report templates, and graceful fallbacks (e.g. branch detection). Weak points: no stated exclusion from the easily-confused /benchmark-models skill, undisclosed shared side effects (CLAUDE.md edits/commits, telemetry, migration) baked into its boilerplate preamble, that preamble.

highlights.summary: Opt-in telemetry (duration/outcome/repo name) posted to a gstack endpoint via the shared preamble; off by default but a baked-in destination.
description: 'Performance regression detection using the browse daemon.' doesn't disclose the preamble's CLAUDE.md edits/commits, migration, or telemetry.
description states purpose ('Performance regression detection...') but no explicit trigger phrasing.
